subject: [RFC PATCH 21/45] mm: page_alloc: prefer reclaim over tainting clean superpageblocks

2026-04-30.patch_summary: This patch restructures the Linux kernel's memory allocation fallback cascade to prefer reclaim and compaction over tainting clean superpageblocks (SPBs). When allocating pages, it first tries to reclaim free pages from existing tainted SPBs before considering clean SPBs. This change helps maintain the homogeneity of clean SPBs by reducing the number of tainted SPBs created during heavy workloads. The patch also updates the slowpath allocation logic to preserve the ALLOC_NOFRAGMENT flag and drop it only as a last resort after reclaim and compaction have failed.
